title: ACR AC — Headache
text: For sudden severe headache (thunderclap): CT head without contrast usually appropriate (rule out SAH). MRA / CTA if non-contrast CT negative and SAH still suspected. For chronic headache without red flags: imaging usually NOT appropriate. Red flags: age > 50, immunocompromised, prior cancer, focal neuro, papilledema, sudden severe.

title: ACR AC — Low back pain
text: For acute uncomplicated low back pain (< 6 weeks, no red flags): imaging usually NOT appropriate. With red flags (cancer history, fracture, infection, cauda equina): MRI lumbar spine without and with contrast usually appropriate. Red flags: progressive neurologic deficit, suspected malignancy, infection, fracture, cauda equina syndrome.

title: ACR AC — Acute non-localized abdominal pain
text: CT abdomen and pelvis with IV contrast usually appropriate as first-line in adults. Ultrasound for women of childbearing age with right lower quadrant pain (appendicitis) or right upper quadrant pain (cholecystitis). MRI for pregnant patients when ultrasound non-diagnostic.

title: ACR AC — Suspected pulmonary embolism
text: Wells score + D-dimer for risk stratification. Low risk + negative D-dimer: imaging NOT appropriate. Moderate/high risk OR positive D-dimer: CTA chest with IV contrast usually appropriate. V/Q scan alternative if CTA contraindicated (renal insufficiency, contrast allergy, pregnancy).

title: ACR AC — Incidental pulmonary nodule
text: Apply Fleischner 2017 criteria for incidental pulmonary nodules found on CT in patients age 35+. For nodules in screening context (high-risk patients in lung-cancer-screening program), apply Lung-RADS 1.1 instead.

title: ACR AC — Hematuria workup
text: Microscopic hematuria with risk factors (smoking, age > 35, chemical exposure): CT urography with and without contrast usually appropriate. Cystoscopy for bladder evaluation. Renal ultrasound + non-contrast CT if iodinated contrast contraindicated.

title: ACR AC — Incidental adrenal mass
text: Mass < 4 cm with homogeneous low attenuation (< 10 HU) on non-contrast CT: likely benign adenoma; no follow-up. Mass > 4 cm OR heterogeneous OR > 10 HU non-contrast: adrenal protocol CT or MRI. Functional testing for pheochromocytoma, hyperaldosteronism, hypercortisolism in selected patients.
